The apostle had no worldly goals in his preaching. Suffering for a good cause should strengthen our commitment to what is right. The message of Christ at first faced much opposition; it was preached with argument, with effort in preaching, and against those who opposed it. Because the content of the apostle's encouragement was true and pure, his way of speaking was honest. The message of Christ is meant to weaken wrong desires, so that people may submit to the power of faith. This is a major reason for being sincere: to consider that God not only sees everything we do, but knows our thoughts from afar, and examines our hearts. And it is from this God who tests our hearts that we must receive our reward. The evidence of the apostle's sincerity was that he avoided flattery and greed. He avoided ambition and empty praise.

Gentleness and kindness greatly recommend religion, and are most in line with God’s gracious treatment of sinners, through and within the message of the gospel. This is how to win people over. We should not only be faithful to our calling as Christians, but also in our specific jobs and relationships. Our great privilege in the gospel is that God has called us to his kingdom and glory. The main duty we have in the gospel is to live a life worthy of God. We should live as becomes those who are called with such an important and holy calling. Our primary focus should be to honor, serve, and please God, and to seek to be worthy of him.

We should receive the word of God with feelings that match its holiness, wisdom, truth, and goodness. People’s words are weak and temporary, like themselves, and sometimes false, foolish, and unreliable; but God's word is holy, wise, just, and faithful. Let us receive and respect it accordingly. The word worked in them to make them examples to others in faith and good works, and in patience under suffering, and in trials for the sake of the message of the gospel. Murder and persecution are hateful to God, and no enthusiasm for anything related to religion can excuse them. Nothing more contributes to a person or people filling up the measure of their sins than opposing the message of the gospel and preventing people’s salvation. The pure message of Christ is disliked by many, and its faithful preaching is hindered in many ways. But those who prevent it from being preached to sinners, to people spiritually dead, do not please God. Those who deny them the Bible have cruel hearts, and are enemies to God's glory and to the salvation of his people.

This world is not a place where we will always be, or stay together for long. In heaven, holy souls will meet and never part again. Even though the apostle could not come to them yet, and thought he might never be able to come, our Lord Jesus Christ will come; nothing can stop that. May God give faithful ministers to all who serve him with their spirit in the message of his Son, and send them to those who are in darkness.
